taskDescription: Arrange the structures in order as the femoral artery travels from the groin to the popliteal fossa.
paragraphs: The femoral artery enters the thigh by passing behind the inguinal ligament at the mid-inguinal point.
It descends through the femoral triangle, bounded by the inguinal ligament, sartorius, and adductor longus.
The artery enters the adductor canal (subsartorial canal), covered by sartorius and the vasto-adductor membrane.
It passes through the adductor hiatus — an opening in adductor magnus near the adductor tubercle.
The artery continues as the popliteal artery in the popliteal fossa behind the knee.
